macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh is sworn in as Federal Reserve Chair at the White House — a politically symbolic ceremony not seen in nearly 40 years, signaling a closer Fed-White House alignment.
primary_drivers: Perceived loss of Fed independence raises long-end term premium and inflation expectations
Warsh historically hawkish on QE/balance sheet but seen as more market-friendly on rates under political pressure to cut
Steeper yield curve and weaker USD on credibility concerns

beneficiaries.rationale: Newmont — largest US-listed gold miner with operating leverage to gold price; benefits from same credibility/inflation-hedge bid as bullion.
Freeport-McMoRan — copper/gold exposure plays the weaker-dollar, reflation-from-politicized-Fed thesis.

headlines: Kevin Warsh takes over as Fed chair today — after first swearing-in at White House in nearly 40 years

macro_events.event_summary: Iran war has pushed US gasoline prices to nearly four-year highs heading into Memorial Day driving season, with Strait of Hormuz risk premium embedded in crude.
primary_drivers: Crude oil price spike from Middle East supply risk / Hormuz transit threat
Refining crack spread expansion as gasoline prices rise faster than crude inputs
Consumer discretionary squeeze from higher fuel costs eating disposable income

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream production leveraged directly to Brent/WTI spike; every $10 in crude adds materially to E&P cash flow. Limited Iran/Hormuz operational exposure vs. European peers.
Pure-play upstream E&P with no refining drag — full leverage to higher crude realizations. Highest beta to oil price among large-cap US producers.

Largest US refiner by capacity; gasoline crack spreads widening into Memorial Day driving season directly expands per-barrel margins. Domestic refiners benefit from elevated retail gasoline even as crude rises.
Pure-play refiner with heavy gasoline yield mix; benefits directly from the gasoline price spike cited in the headline. Gulf Coast assets insulated from Middle East operational risk.
Refining + midstream exposure capturing both crack spread widening and product transport demand during peak driving season.
Permian pure-play with low breakevens; sustained crude above $90 drives outsized FCF and variable dividend payouts.
High-leverage US producer (Permian-weighted) with elevated oil-price sensitivity; debt paydown accelerates at higher crude, equity rerates.
Oilfield services see activity uplift as sustained high prices incentivize US shale completions; Halliburton's North American pressure pumping mix is most levered to a domestic drilling response.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of operating costs and Southwest is unhedged structurally relative to legacy peers; sustained crude spike compresses margins directly into peak summer travel bookings.
Owns its own refinery (Trainer) which partially offsets, but jet crack spreads still pressure unit costs; consumer discretionary travel demand also at risk if gas prices crimp household budgets.
Large international long-haul exposure with high jet fuel burn; unhedged fuel costs flow straight to earnings.
Low-income consumer base most sensitive to gas-pump-as-tax; historical correlation between $4+ gasoline and Walmart traffic/basket compression. Diesel also raises freight costs.
Dollar General's rural/low-income customer spends disproportionate share of income on fuel; sustained pump pain directly compresses comp sales as seen in prior oil spikes.
Cruise operator with massive bunker fuel exposure (heavy fuel oil tracks crude); also faces discretionary travel demand risk from squeezed consumer.
Diesel and jet fuel are major cost inputs; fuel surcharges lag and don't fully offset, especially in Ground segment during peak season.
E-commerce shipper of heavy/bulky goods — last-mile fuel costs and consumer discretionary pressure both negative; pet category historically discretionary-trimmed when gas spikes.

headlines: Iran war leaves U.S. gas prices at highest levels in nearly four years ahead of Memorial Day
